message:
feat(cli): add AI agent interface for programmatic graph analysis

Add command-line flags enabling AI agents and scripts to access the
graph analysis capabilities without parsing raw JSONL issue files.

New CLI flags:

--robot-help
  Prints documentation explaining the available metrics and their
  interpretation for AI agents. Describes:
  - PageRank: measures "blocking power" (fundamental dependencies)
  - Betweenness: measures "bottleneck status" (connects clusters)
  - CriticalPathScore: heuristic for blocking chain depth
  - Hubs/Authorities: HITS algorithm relationship scores
  - Cycles: circular dependency detection (unhealthy state indicator)

--robot-insights
  Outputs comprehensive graph analysis as JSON to stdout, including:
  - Top 50 items for each metric category (bottlenecks, keystones,
    influencers, hubs, authorities)
  - All detected dependency cycles
  - Cluster density measurement
  - Full GraphStats with raw metric maps

JSON output considerations:
- Changed insights.go to use make([]InsightItem, 0) instead of nil
  slice declaration, ensuring empty arrays serialize as [] not null
- Proper indentation (2-space) for human readability
- Errors written to stderr to keep stdout clean for piping

This enables workflows like:
  bv --robot-insights | jq '.bottlenecks[:5]'
  bv --robot-insights > analysis.json

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1,10 +1,12 @@
 package main
 
 import (
+	"encoding/json"
 	"flag"
 	"fmt"
 	"os"
 
+	"beads_viewer/pkg/analysis"
 	"beads_viewer/pkg/export"
 	"beads_viewer/pkg/loader"
 	"beads_viewer/pkg/ui"
@@ -17,6 +19,8 @@ func main() {
 	help := flag.Bool("help", false, "Show help")
 	versionFlag := flag.Bool("version", false, "Show version")
 	exportFile := flag.String("export-md", "", "Export issues to a Markdown file (e.g., report.md)")
+	robotHelp := flag.Bool("robot-help", false, "Show AI agent help")
+	robotInsights := flag.Bool("robot-insights", false, "Output graph analysis and insights as JSON for AI agents")
 	flag.Parse()
 
 	if *help {
@@ -26,6 +30,27 @@ func main() {
 		os.Exit(0)
 	}
 
+	if *robotHelp {
+		fmt.Println("bv (Beads Viewer) AI Agent Interface")
+		fmt.Println("====================================")
+		fmt.Println("This tool provides structural analysis of the issue tracker graph (DAG).")
+		fmt.Println("Use these commands to understand project state without parsing raw JSONL.")
+		fmt.Println("")
+		fmt.Println("Commands:")
+		fmt.Println("  --robot-insights")
+		fmt.Println("      Outputs a JSON object containing deep graph analysis.")
+		fmt.Println("      Key metrics explained:")
+		fmt.Println("      - PageRank: Measures 'blocking power'. High score = Fundamental dependency.")
+		fmt.Println("      - Betweenness: Measures 'bottleneck status'. High score = Connects disparate clusters.")
+		fmt.Println("      - CriticalPathScore: Heuristic for depth. High score = Blocking a long chain of work.")
+		fmt.Println("      - Hubs/Authorities: HITS algorithm scores for dependency relationships.")
+		fmt.Println("      - Cycles: Lists of circular dependencies (unhealthy state).")
+		fmt.Println("")
+		fmt.Println("  --export-md <file>")
+		fmt.Println("      Generates a readable status report with Mermaid.js visualizations.")
+		os.Exit(0)
+	}
+
 	if *versionFlag {
 		fmt.Printf("bv %s\n", version.Version)
 		os.Exit(0)
@@ -34,11 +59,26 @@ func main() {
 	// Load issues from current directory
 	issues, err := loader.LoadIssues("")
 	if err != nil {
-		fmt.Printf("Error loading beads: %v\n", err)
-		fmt.Println("Make sure you are in a project initialized with 'bd init'.")
+		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error loading beads: %v\n", err)
+		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Make sure you are in a project initialized with 'bd init'.")
 		os.Exit(1)
 	}
 
+	if *robotInsights {
+		analyzer := analysis.NewAnalyzer(issues)
+		stats := analyzer.Analyze()
+		// Generate top 50 lists for summary, but full stats are included in the struct
+		insights := stats.GenerateInsights(50)
+
+		encoder := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
+		encoder.SetIndent("", "  ")
+		if err := encoder.Encode(insights); err != nil {
+			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error encoding insights: %v\n", err)
+			os.Exit(1)
+		}
+		os.Exit(0)
+	}
+
 	if *exportFile != "" {
 		fmt.Printf("Exporting to %s...\n", *exportFile)
 		if err := export.SaveMarkdownToFile(issues, *exportFile); err != nil {
